analysis.overall_assessment: Your autonomic function shows good stability with a strong average HRV of 68.6ms and well-controlled resting HR of 47.4 bpm, suggesting effective POTS management. However, the dramatic HRV crash on 4/25 (dropping to 40ms) followed by poor sleep (6.9h) indicates your system still has vulnerability to autonomic destabilization. Temperature deviation averaging 0.0°C suggests stable thyroid/immune status currently.
whats_going_well: Excellent sleep scores averaging 84.9/100 with consistent deep sleep (1.2-1.7h nightly) supporting immune system regulation for Hashimoto's management
Strong autonomic recovery capacity demonstrated by HRV rebounding from 40ms on 4/25 back to 62-68ms by 4/24-4/26, showing resilience
Well-paced activity with 19 workouts over 14 days and 6,678 average steps - appropriate grading for POTS without triggering sustained tachycardia
areas_to_improve: Address the HRV volatility pattern - the 40ms reading on 4/25 with elevated RHR (51 bpm) suggests autonomic stress that needs investigation of triggers
Sleep duration inconsistency ranging from 6.5-8.5 hours may be contributing to autonomic instability - aim for more consistent 7.5-8 hour target
Consider scaling activity on low-HRV days - the 7,871 steps on 4/25 when HRV was critically low (40ms) may have worsened autonomic dysfunction
analysis.autonomic_assessment: Your baseline autonomic function is remarkably strong with HRV averaging 68.6ms and RHR controlled at 47.4 bpm, indicating good POTS stability. However, the sharp HRV drop to 40ms on 4/25 with corresponding RHR spike to 51 bpm represents significant sympathetic overdrive that warrants close monitoring for triggers.
analysis.thyroid_immune_signals: Temperature deviations averaging 0.0°C suggest stable thyroid hormone levels without active Hashimoto's flare. Deep sleep averaging 1.3 hours provides adequate immune system recovery time, though the shorter sleep duration on 4/25 (6.9h, 1.2h deep) may have contributed to that day's autonomic instability.
analysis.vestibular_readiness: High readiness scores (83-89 except 4/25 at 72) indicate good capacity for vestibular rehabilitation most days. The low readiness of 72 on 4/25 correlating with HRV crash suggests that day required scaled-back vestibular exercises to avoid overwhelming compensation mechanisms.
analysis.focus_for_next_week: Implement HRV-guided activity modification - on days when HRV drops below 55ms, reduce steps to under 5,000 and skip intensive vestibular rehab to prevent autonomic crashes like the 4/25 episode.
